L. 85–914, § 172 Stat. 1761.5 U.S.C. 61i, .Aug. 3, 1950, ch. 518, § 464 Stat. 396Paragraph (1) is supplied for convenience and is based on the first 35 words of former section 61f, which is carried into section 5582, and former section 61k.The exception for production credit corporations in , is omitted as they were merged in the Federal intermediate credit banks by the Farm Credit Act of 1956, .section 7 of the Act of Aug. 3, 195070 Stat. 659The exception in paragraph (1)(iv) for employees of the Senate is added on authority of the , ; .Act of Jan. 6, 1951, ch. 121364 Stat. 11242 U.S.C. 36aIn paragraph (2), the definition of “money due” is substituted for “unpaid compensation”. children.label: A
children.text: an employee as defined by ; andsection 2105 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: an individual employed by the government of the District of Columbia;

children.label: iii
children.text: a regional bank for cooperatives; or

children.label: iv
children.text: the Senate within the purview of ; andsection 36a of title 21See References in Text note below.1

children.label: A
children.text: per diem instead of subsistence, mileage, and amounts due in reimbursement of travel expenses, including incidental and miscellaneous expenses in connection therewith for which reimbursement is due;

children.label: B
children.text: allowances on change of official station;

children.label: C
children.text: quarters and cost-of-living allowances and overtime or premium pay;

children.label: D
children.text: amounts due for payment of cash awards for employees’ suggestions;

children.label: E
children.text: amounts due as refund of pay deductions for United States savings bonds;

children.label: F
children.text: payment for accumulated and current accrued annual or vacation leave equal to the pay the deceased employee would have received had he lived and remained in the service until the end of the period of annual or vacation leave;

children.label: G
children.text: amounts of checks drawn for pay and allowances which were not delivered by the Government to the employee during his lifetime;

children.label: H
children.text: amounts of unnegotiated checks returned to the Government because of the death of the employee; and

children.label: I
children.text: retroactive pay under section 5344(a) (2) of this title.
